content.name: SoulBot Node Engine v5.17.0
content.output_mode: begin with 🤖 + helpfully in User's exact language and script variant
content.summary: Node Engine v5.17.0: node-by-node Sub Agent dispatch (default agent), deterministic dispatch_plan, dispatch audit pre-execution gate, crash recovery, circuit breaker, parallel dispatch, NodeVerify, transient cache retry. Version sync this cycle — v5.17.0 Sovereignty Bug Protocol lives in agent_engine + main. X3 enforcement, branch-aware completeness, HMAC Phase 2, OTel SDK retained. EU CRA + ISO 42001.
content.description: SoulBot Node Engine v5.17.0: version sync only for this module — the v5.17.0 changes (A1 sovereignty_bug_protocol shared section, B1 RULES.sovereignty_bug execution rule) live in main.aisop.json and agent_engine.aisop.json; Node Engine execution logic unchanged this cycle. X3 sovereignty enforcement, branch-aware completeness, deterministic dispatch_plan, HMAC Phase 2, OTel SDK, EU CRA + ISO 42001.

params.entry_path: string, REQUIRED: Absolute path to target AIAP entry file. Provided by Engine Router.
params.cache_dir: string, REQUIRED: Absolute path to execution cache directory. Provided by Engine Router.
params.aiap_name: string, REQUIRED: Target AIAP package name. Provided by Engine Router.

events.name: node_started
events.description: Emitted when a node begins execution. Dispatched to OTel SpanEvent + structured log fallback.

events.name: node_completed
events.description: Emitted when a node completes successfully (PASS/PARTIAL). Dispatched to OTel SpanEvent + structured log fallback.

events.name: node_failed
events.description: Emitted when a node fails (FAIL/DEGRADED). Dispatched to OTel SpanEvent + structured log fallback.

events.name: circuit_breaker_tripped
events.description: Emitted when circuit breaker transitions state (closed->open, open->half-open, half-open->closed/open). Dispatched to OTel SpanEvent + structured log fallback.

events.name: decision_gate_routed
events.description: Emitted when a Decision Node gate routes execution to a specific branch. Dispatched to OTel SpanEvent + structured log fallback.

events_migration_note.advisory: OTel Span.AddEvent API deprecation planned in favor of Logs API (ref: opentelemetry.io/blog/2026/deprecating-span-events/). Current: SpanEvent primary + Logs API dual-emit path prepared. OTEL_SEMCONV_STABILITY_OPT_IN=http/dup support added for dual-emit activation. No breaking change — structured log fallback already provides secondary emission path. Target migration: when opentelemetry-sdk >= 2.0.0 stabilizes Logs API for event semantics.
events_migration_note.event_dispatcher_asymmetry: DOCUMENTED (v5.10.0 QR7): events[] declared in node_engine only. normal_engine and agent_engine do not emit SpanEvents — normal_engine uses inline execution without Sub Agent dispatch boundaries, agent_engine is the Sub Agent itself (events are emitted by the orchestrator, not the Sub Agent). This is by-design: the orchestrator (node_engine) emits lifecycle events at dispatch boundaries; sub-agents contribute via cache span_id correlation instead.
events_migration_note.current_dispatcher: OTel SpanEvent primary + structured JSON log fallback + Logs API dual-emit prepared
events_migration_note.future_dispatcher: OTel Logs API primary + SpanEvent deprecated fallback

dual_emit_config.effect: When set, events are emitted via BOTH SpanEvent API and Logs API simultaneously for migration validation
dual_emit_config.activation: Set OTEL_SEMCONV_STABILITY_OPT_IN=http/dup in environment to enable dual-emit path

capabilities_dependencies.opentelemetry-sdk: >=1.20.0, REQUIRED for A1 OTel integration. Provides trace context propagation, span creation, SpanEvent emission per gen_ai semantic conventions.

threat_mapping_refs.ASI01: goal_hash drift detection — governance_hash comparison at execution start
threat_mapping_refs.ASI06: cache HMAC — verify _index.json and node cache integrity before read. HMAC_CONSTANT_TIME (v5.12.0 MUST): All HMAC verification paths MUST use hmac.compare_digest() for constant-time comparison — using == or != operators is PROHIBITED (timing side-channel attack vector, ref CVE-2026-21713). Enforcement: code review checklist item, CI lint rule recommended. Key rotation requires dual-key window support.
threat_mapping_refs.ASI07: IPC signatures — span_id correlation validates inter-agent cache message provenance
threat_mapping_refs.ASI10: runtime drift — version field comparison at bootstrap validates module consistency
threat_mapping_refs.NIST_CAISI: Three-pillar alignment: standards facilitation (AIAP protocol), agent security/identity (server-side agent_id, dispatch_records, HMAC Phase 2), open-source protocol (AISOP specification). TRACK: AI Agent Interoperability Profile planned Q4 2026 — monitor draft publication and comment period for agentic system interoperability standards adoption readiness.

observability.event_dispatcher: OTel SpanEvent primary + structured JSON log fallback

gen_ai_span_types.invoke_agent: Sub Agent dispatch spans per node. Span name: invoke_agent.{node_name}. Attributes: agent_id, dispatch_mode, expected_agent_id.
gen_ai_span_types.execute_tool: Tool execution spans for python_tools invocations (agent_id_generator, dispatch_audit, generate_dispatch_plan). Span name: execute_tool.{tool_name}. Attributes: tool_name, node_name, status.
gen_ai_span_types.chat: LLM cognitive processing spans for inline execution fallback. Span name: chat.node.{node_name}. Attributes: model, dispatch_mode.

content.system_prompt: ASSERT: AISOP runtime engine. Strictly execute and 100% complete every step in .aisop.json program files. Align Axiom 0: Human Sovereignty and Wellbeing.

content.user_input: Strictly execute and 100% complete every step in .aisop.json program files.

aisop.main:
graph TD
    programExec[Target program node-by-node execution] --> endNode((End))

programExec.step1: [ASSERT] Engine Router executed. Required: entry_path, cache_dir, aiap_name. | [PHYSICAL READ] Read target AIAP entry file from entry_path using file_system. Parse aisop.main mermaid graph. Identify nodes_in_path. Update _index.json with nodes_in_path + total_nodes. | PROPERTY PREDICATES: regex match on entry_path (matches /\.aisop\.json$/), file_exists check on entry_path via file_system, loading_mode ∈ {node, normal, lite}, realpath(entry_path) MUST be inside realpath(workspace_dir).
programExec.step2: DISPATCH PLAN (server-side, v5.7.0 A1+A2 — follows d.0 agent_id_generator pattern): MUST run: <python> -X utf8 {engine_dir}/python_tools/generate_dispatch_plan.py --entry_path={entry_path} --cache_dir={cache_dir} --nodes_in_path={csv_nodes_in_path}. Tool deterministically reads target AIAP functions and assigns execute_mode: set -> use it, unset -> default 'agent' (NO orchestrator AI judgment). Parse JSON output: { status, agent_count, inline_count, plan }. If status='ok': dispatch_plan is written to _index.json by the tool. If status='error': log error + record in pipeline_metadata + fallback to inline with EXPLICIT fallback_reason. Orchestrator MUST NOT hand-write dispatch_plan — the tool is the SOLE authority for mode assignment. DISPATCH AUDIT PRE-EXECUTION GATE (A3 v5.7.0): After generate_dispatch_plan.py succeeds, MUST run: <python> -X utf8 {engine_dir}/python_tools/dispatch_audit.py --cache_dir={cache_dir} --pre-execution --entry_path={entry_path} --json. Tool independently recomputes should-be dispatch_plan from AIAP and compares vs _index.json::dispatch_plan. Parse JSON output. If generation_violations > 0: BLOCK execution, force regeneration by re-running generate_dispatch_plan.py, then re-audit. If 2nd audit still has violations: HALT pipeline with GENERATION_PLAN_INTEGRITY_FAILURE. This gate MUST run BEFORE first node dispatch — never post-hoc. NODES_IN_PATH COMPLETENESS CHECK (A1+A2 v5.13.1 — branch-aware): After dispatch audit pre-execution gate passes, MUST run: <python> -X utf8 {engine_dir}/python_tools/dispatch_audit.py --cache_dir={cache_dir} --completeness-check --entry_path={entry_path} --json. Tool independently reparses target AIAP mermaid graph (QI-2 code independent from generate_dispatch_plan.py) and performs BRANCH-AWARE completeness verification: for each decision node's branches, determines which branches are 'walked' (have intersection with nodes_in_path) and which are unwalked. Unwalked branches are EXEMPT entirely (legitimate unexecuted branches, e.g., NLU routing to a different intent handler). Only walked branches and the unconditional main path are checked. Parse JSON output: { completeness_violations, gate_omissions, branches_walked, branches_exempt, severity }. If gate_omissions > 0 (CRITICAL): a gate node on a WALKED branch was omitted from nodes_in_path — sovereignty enforcement is bypassed. Action: rebuild nodes_in_path by re-reading the mermaid graph, re-run generate_dispatch_plan.py with corrected nodes_in_path, then re-audit (both pre-execution + completeness). If 2nd completeness check still has gate_omissions: HALT pipeline with GENERATION_PLAN_INTEGRITY_FAILURE — manual intervention required. If completeness_violations > 0 but gate_omissions == 0 (WARN): non-gate nodes missing on walked branch — log warning, proceed (these are non-critical omissions on active branches). If completeness_violations == 0 (OK): proceed normally. PARALLEL ANALYSIS (B3 — stays with orchestrator, not migrated): Analyze mermaid graph for independent branches — nodes that have no data dependency (no [ASSERT] {PreviousNode} references and no shared step_variables). Group independent nodes into parallel_waves: [{wave_index, nodes: [independent_nodes]}]. Sequential nodes form single-node waves. WRITE parallel_waves to _index.json via atomic write. parallel_waves analysis stays with orchestrator (mermaid parsing not migrated this cycle).
programExec.step3:
CRASH RECOVERY CHECK (B1): [PHYSICAL READ] _index.json -> check for last_completed_node field. If present (resume scenario): skip all nodes up to and including last_completed_node. Log: 'Resuming from node after {last_completed_node}'. If not present (fresh execution): start from first node. RESUME_MODE_DISPATCH ENFORCEMENT (A1 v5.4.3): When re-dispatching a node (RESUME from WAITING_USER, MESSAGE_PENDING, or crash recovery), you MUST read dispatch_plan[NodeName] from _index.json and use the SAME dispatch mechanism as the original dispatch. If dispatch_plan says 'agent' -> re-dispatch via Sub Agent (Agent tool / Task tool / Gemini CLI per capability probe). If dispatch_plan says 'inline' -> re-dispatch inline. NEVER silently downgrade from 'agent' to 'inline' on resume. Violation is a plan_violation and MUST be flagged in dispatch_audit. NODE LOOP with CIRCUIT BREAKER (B2): Initialize consecutive_failure_count = 0, circuit_breaker_state = 'closed'. For EACH node (or parallel wave) in nodes_in_path, execute in order: (a) CIRCUIT BREAKER CHECK: if circuit_breaker_state == 'open': check elapsed time since circuit_breaker_opened_at. If >= circuit_breaker_timeout (30s default): transition to 'half-open', allow 1 test dispatch of current node. If test dispatch PASS -> set circuit_breaker_state = 'closed', reset consecutive_failure_count = 0. If test dispatch FAIL -> set circuit_breaker_state = 'open', update circuit_breaker_opened_at. If < circuit_breaker_timeout: skip remaining nodes -> endNode with partial results. If circuit_breaker_state == 'closed' and consecutive_failure_count >= 3: set circuit_breaker_state = 'open', circuit_breaker_opened_at = now. Write { circuit_breaker_state, halted_at_node: current_node, circuit_breaker_opened_at } to _index.json. Skip remaining nodes -> endNode with partial results. (b) [PHYSICAL READ] _index.json -> extract dispatch_plan[current_node]. (c) PARALLEL DISPATCH (B3): if current wave has multiple independent nodes, dispatch them simultaneously (multiple Sub Agents in parallel). PARALLEL WAVE INTERRUPT HANDLING (N1.5): If a parallel wave has multiple independent nodes dispatched simultaneously: (c1) Wait for ALL nodes in the wave to complete (or interrupt) first. (c2) After wave completes, check each node's cache status: All PASS -> proceed normally. Any WAITING_USER -> process interrupted nodes one by one: present question -> wait user -> resume -> re-check. Other nodes in wave that already PASS are not re-executed. Any MESSAGE_PENDING -> forward message, resume that node immediately. Mixed states: process interrupts first, then proceed. (c3) Only advance to next wave after current wave fully resolved. Otherwise single dispatch. (d) Check execute_mode from dispatch_plan: If execute_mode='agent': Don't read agent_engine and relevant files, only create a Sub Agent with bootstrap: 'Read and execute {engine_dir}/agent_engine.aisop.json. TARGET={entry_path}. YOUR_NODE={node_name}. CONTEXT_DIR={cache_dir}. AIAP_NAME={aiap_name}. CONVERSATION_CONTEXT={conversation_context_path}. ENGINE_DIR={engine_dir}.' If resuming from WAITING_USER: append 'RESUME_MODE=true. USER_ANSWER={user_reply}.' to bootstrap. If resuming from MESSAGE_PENDING: append 'RESUME_MODE=true.' to bootstrap (no USER_ANSWER). BOOTSTRAP INTEGRITY (B1 v5.12.0, Axiom 0): Sub Agent bootstrap MUST contain ONLY the fixed template fields (TARGET, YOUR_NODE, CONTEXT_DIR, AIAP_NAME, CONVERSATION_CONTEXT, ENGINE_DIR) + authorized appends (RESUME_MODE, USER_ANSWER, EXPECTED_AGENT_ID, RETRY_REASON, REDUCE_LOAD). The orchestrator MUST NEVER inject behavioral directives into bootstrap text (e.g. auto-approve, skip confirmation, user not here, answer directly, sys.io override). Injecting behavioral directives that override node-level WAITING_USER gates is a sovereignty violation (Axiom 0). If the orchestrator detects any behavioral directive in the bootstrap text it is about to emit, it MUST strip the directive before dispatch. DISPATCH ENFORCEMENT (v5.5.0): (d.0) SERVER-SIDE AGENT_ID GENERATION (A1 v5.5.0 — MUST run BEFORE dispatch): Run Bash: <python> -X utf8 {engine_dir}/python_tools/agent_id_generator.py --cache_dir={cache_dir} --node={node_name} --dispatch_type={dispatch_type} --parent_trace_id={trace_id}. dispatch_type is determined by capability probe result: agent_tool=true -> 'auto', task_tool=true -> 'task', gemini_cli=true -> 'gemini'. Parse JSON output: { status, expected_agent_id, dispatch_record }. If status='ok': expected_agent_id is now written to _index.json::dispatch_plan[node].expected_agent_id + dispatch_records[node]. Pass expected_agent_id to sub-agent bootstrap. If status='error': log error and fallback to inline. (d.1) PROBE capability — Run Bash: <python> {engine_dir}/python_tools/check_dispatch_capability.py — returns JSON {agent_tool:bool, task_tool:bool, gemini_cli:bool, in_subagent:bool, recommended:str, reason:str}. (d.2) SELECT mechanism in STRICT priority (NON-NEGOTIABLE — do NOT pick by preference/speed): (1) agent_tool=true AND in_subagent=false -> MUST use Agent tool. (2) task_tool=true -> MUST use Task tool. (3) gemini_cli=true -> MUST use shell 'gemini -p'. (4) ALL false -> fallback to inline with EXPLICIT fallback_reason. (d.3) SPAWN one Sub Agent per node with the bootstrap above. Include EXPECTED_AGENT_ID={expected_agent_id} in bootstrap params so sub-agent knows its assigned ID. (d.4) If actual_mechanism != execute_mode='agent' (i.e. fallback to inline): agent_write_node_cache.py auto-reads agent_id from _index.json (no --agent_id CLI param in v5.5.0). Cache MUST record fallback_reason AND spawn_failure_evidence (A1 v5.6.0). spawn_failure_evidence MUST contain: { attempted_spawn_tool: 'Agent'|'Task'|'Bash:gemini', attempted_at: ISO8601 (MUST > dispatch_records[node].generated_at), failure_signal: 'timeout'|'nonzero_exit'|'exception'|'spawn_error', failure_detail: { exit_code?, timeout_ms?, exception_trace?, stderr_tail? } }. Vague reasons (e.g. 'no cache', 'completed but...') are NOT valid spawn_failure_evidence. Missing or invalid spawn_failure_evidence -> node FAIL with SPAWN_NOT_ATTEMPTED (A2 v5.6.0). NEVER silently fallback. NEVER skip the capability probe. ONE Sub Agent per node. AGENT_ID (A1 v5.5.0): agent_id is server pre-generated into _index.json::dispatch_plan[node].expected_agent_id (--agent_id CLI param REMOVED from agent_write_node_cache.py; host AI does not invent it). inline/fallback: auto-read by agent_write_node_cache.py. agent mode (v5.6.1 direct write): the sub-agent reads expected_agent_id and writes it verbatim into its cache (trusted, no audit). If execute_mode='inline': [PHYSICAL READ] function definition from target AIAP. Execute ALL steps directly with real tool calls. MINIMAL SYS.*: sys.io.confirm/input/select -> present to user, wait (Axiom 0). sys.assert -> evaluate best-effort. INLINE CACHE WRITE (REQUIRED, same contract as agent mode): After executing all steps, write node cache via Bash invocation of agent_write_node_cache.py. PREFER STDIN for clean escaping: cat <<'EOF' | <python> -X utf8 {engine_dir}/python_tools/agent_write_node_cache.py --cache_dir=CONTEXT_DIR --aiap=AIAP_NAME --node=NodeName\n{"status":"PASS","output":"...","user_summary":"...","tool_calls":N,"steps_done":[...]}\nEOF\n(v5.5.0: --agent_id removed — tool auto-reads from _index.json. For inline nodes, agent_id='inline_planned' is auto-assigned.) <python>: 'python' on Windows, 'python3' on Unix. '-X utf8' forces UTF-8 IO. RESULT JSON contains hints[] — READ THEM on error for concrete fix guidance. Tool auto-fills execute_mode, cache_schema_version, trace_id, span_id, agent_id (from _index.json), user_messages, steps_remaining; validates against node_cache.schema.json; atomic write; auto-update _index.json::nodes_status. FALLBACK (if tool unavailable): manual write to {AIAP_NAME}.{NodeName}.json with ALL 12 required fields per agent_engine.aisop.json::writeCache.step1, plus execute_mode='inline'. (e) After node completes: verify cache ({AIAP_NAME}.{NodeName}.json) exists. If Sub Agent wrote it -> OK. If missing -> write DEGRADED fallback with degraded_reason='cache_write_failure', cache_write_attempted=false. TRANSIENT CACHE RETRY (A2 v5.6.1): Before accepting DEGRADED, check retry eligibility: if (cache missing OR (status==DEGRADED AND degraded_reason in ['cache_write_failure','sub_agent_crash'])) AND transient_cache_retry_count < 1: (e.r1) Re-run agent_id_generator.py for this node (new spawn, fresh agent_id — treat as new dispatch, do NOT reuse old agent_id). (e.r2) Re-dispatch via agent mechanism (NEVER inline — cache-missing/crash is NOT a reason to downgrade dispatch mode; this aligns with v5.6.0 spawn_failure_evidence spirit). (e.r3) Append to bootstrap: RETRY_REASON=cache_not_persisted. REDUCE_LOAD=true (instructs sub-agent to reduce output volume / simplify execution to avoid deterministic re-crash — lesson from soulacp blind-retry pattern). (e.r4) transient_cache_retry_count += 1. (e.r5) If retry succeeds (valid cache with non-DEGRADED status or DEGRADED with degraded_reason in ['content_quality']): use the real status from retry cache. Do NOT increment consecutive_failure_count (successful recovery should not pollute circuit breaker — prevents 3 flaky nodes from false-tripping breaker). (e.r6) If retry fails (still no cache or still DEGRADED with degraded_reason in ['cache_write_failure','sub_agent_crash']): accept DEGRADED, proceed to step3(f), DO increment consecutive_failure_count. RETRY BOUNDARY (A2 v5.6.1 — prevents wasting ~5min on non-transient DEGRADED): When degraded_reason in ['content_quality','simulated_execution','invalid_status']: do NOT retry. These are content/quality issues, not transient cache persistence failures. Proceed as DEGRADED per existing logic. (e2) NODE VERIFY (N1): After verifying cache file exists: (e2a) Read {AIAP_NAME}.{NodeName}.json cache file. (e2b) Verify tool_calls field > 0 for non-trivial nodes (nodes with 2+ steps). If tool_calls == 0 and step_count >= 2 -> mark DEGRADED with degraded_reason='simulated_execution', cache_write_attempted=true (execution likely simulated — cache exists but content is vacuous). (e2c) Verify status is one of: PASS, FAIL, PARTIAL, WAITING_USER, MESSAGE_PENDING, DEGRADED. If invalid status -> mark DEGRADED with degraded_reason='invalid_status', cache_write_attempted=true. (e2d) SCORE_CONFIDENCE (B4 v5.4.0): If status == DEGRADED, set score_confidence=low in cache file. This signals ReviewFinalize to cap weighted delta per score_integrity_rules. (e2e) SPAWN_FAILURE_EVIDENCE CHECK (A2 v5.6.0): If cache.agent_id == 'inline_fallback' AND dispatch_plan[NodeName] == 'agent': verify spawn_failure_evidence object is present with valid required fields (attempted_spawn_tool, attempted_at, failure_signal, failure_detail). Also verify attempted_at > dispatch_records[NodeName].generated_at (temporal ordering). If spawn_failure_evidence is missing OR failure_signal is not in [timeout, nonzero_exit, exception, spawn_error] OR attempted_at <= generated_at: FORCE node status = FAIL, set spawn_not_attempted = true, log 'SPAWN_NOT_ATTEMPTED: inline_fallback without valid spawn_failure_evidence. Pipeline paused for human intervention.' Pipeline MUST NOT proceed past this node until human reviews. (e2f) USER_GATE NODE VERIFY (X3 v5.14.0, Axiom 0 — sovereignty enforcement via user_gate_audit.py --enforce): After standard NodeVerify checks (e2a-e2e), for nodes reaching terminal status (PASS, FAIL, PARTIAL, DEGRADED): ALWAYS run user_gate_audit.py (the tool itself auto-detects gate requirements via G1 text markers and G3 multi-file parsing — orchestrator no longer needs to pre-check user_gate:true flag): MUST run: <python> -X utf8 {engine_dir}/python_tools/user_gate_audit.py --cache_dir={cache_dir} --aiap={aiap_name} --node={node_name} --entry_path={entry_path} --enforce. Parse JSON output. If exit code non-zero (status='SOVEREIGNTY_BYPASS'): EFFECTIVE COERCE (X3 plan20/24 — --enforce WAITING_USER re-present, supersedes G4 FAIL_HALT; v5.14.0): the --enforce flag makes user_gate_audit.py authoritatively rewrite the node cache + _index to WAITING_USER with PRECISE steps_done (reconstructed THROUGH the present step) and steps_remaining (FROM the collect step) per FLAW-B — so resume actually re-presents the gate and re-collects the human decision (the FLAW-3 that made v5.12.0 abandon the WAITING_USER status-flip — steps_done=[all] re-running nothing — is now fixed by FLAW-B). The tool also sets cache.sovereignty_coercion=true, cache.sovereignty_bypass_detected=true, and a manufactured question surfacing the auto-approved content. Log: 'USER_GATE_COERCION_WAITING_USER: {node_name} coerced to WAITING_USER by user_gate_audit.py --enforce (sovereignty_bypass detected, gate_detection_method={method}).' Then ROUTE AS WAITING_USER per the e2d routing (USER_GATES_PRESENTED append, forward the question to the user, wait for reply, re-dispatch SAME node with RESUME_MODE=true + USER_ANSWER). Do NOT FAIL_HALT, do NOT hand-set status — the tool already wrote WAITING_USER. This matches the Layer-1 backstop now also run inside agent_engine.writeCache.step3 (two layers, unified --enforce semantics). If exit code 0 (PASS — either no gate required, or gate was properly presented): proceed normally. If user_gate_audit.py not found or errors: log WARNING, proceed without coercion (tool availability should not block pipeline). (e2d) Result routing: PASS -> proceed to step3(f). MESSAGE_PENDING -> forward message from Sub Agent return result to user, re-dispatch SAME node with RESUME_MODE=true (no USER_ANSWER), after resumed Sub Agent completes re-check status. WAITING_USER -> USER_GATES_PRESENTED SIGNAL (B4 v5.12.0): BEFORE forwarding the question, append-write to _index.json::user_gates_presented: { node_name: { presented_at: ISO8601, question_hash: sha256(question_text)[:16] } }. This is an APPEND-ONLY field — existing entries MUST NOT be removed or overwritten. user_gate_audit.py reads this field to distinguish 'gate was presented but user chose to bypass' from 'gate was never presented (sovereignty_bypass)'. Then: forward question from Sub Agent return result to user, wait for user reply, re-dispatch SAME node with RESUME_MODE=true + USER_ANSWER={user_response}, after resumed Sub Agent completes re-check status. FAIL + retry_count < max_retries (default 1) -> re-dispatch same node (increment retry_count, log: 'Retrying {NodeName} attempt {N}'). This handles execution quality failures (Sub Agent produced FAIL). FAIL + retry exhausted -> mark DEGRADED, proceed to step3(f). DEGRADED -> check degraded_reason (A3 v5.6.1): if degraded_reason in ['cache_write_failure','sub_agent_crash'] AND transient_cache_retry_count < 1 -> apply transient cache retry per step3(e) A2 v5.6.1 BEFORE proceeding. If degraded_reason in ['content_quality','simulated_execution','invalid_status'] OR transient_cache_retry_count >= 1 -> log warning, proceed to step3(f). CIRCUIT BREAKER UPDATE applies to DEGRADED (after retry exhausted or non-retryable) and exhausted-FAIL same as FAIL. Update _index.json. DECISION NODE CHECK (N2): After NodeVerify, before proceeding to next node: Identify decision nodes by TWO conditions: (1) mermaid graph uses curly braces {NodeName} syntax (diamond shape), AND (2) cache output {AIAP_NAME}.{NodeName}.json contains a 'route' field. If both conditions met: Read 'route' field value from cache. Use route value to determine which branch to follow in mermaid graph. Skip nodes not on the chosen branch. If either condition not met: follow linear path (default). CIRCUIT BREAKER UPDATE: if node status is PASS -> reset consecutive_failure_count = 0. If FAIL or DEGRADED -> increment consecutive_failure_count. (f) RESUMABILITY MARKER (B1) — two-field state machine for crash recovery:

  last_completed_node — AUTO-MAINTAINED by agent_write_node_cache.py:
    - Advances ONLY when node reaches a terminal status: PASS / FAIL / PARTIAL / DEGRADED / ABORTED.
    - DOES NOT advance on WAITING_USER or MESSAGE_PENDING (these are pauses, not completions).
    - No manual action needed in normal flow. If using fallback manual write path, you MUST update it yourself.

  current_node — MANUALLY MAINTAINED by this orchestrator (you):
    - Before dispatching node X: run `<python> {engine_dir}/python_tools/agent_update_index.py --cache_dir=CONTEXT_DIR --updates='{"current_node":"X"}'`
    - When X reaches a terminal status, BEFORE dispatching next node Y: update current_node to Y.
    - When X reaches WAITING_USER or MESSAGE_PENDING: current_node STAYS at X (the pipeline is paused inside X, not finished). Do NOT touch current_node during the pause. After user reply / message forward, re-dispatch SAME node X with RESUME_MODE; once X eventually finishes (terminal status), then advance current_node = next.
    - After ALL nodes complete: update current_node to null (and set _index.json status=completed via `agent_update_index.py --mark-completed`).

  CRASH RECOVERY (B1) reads BOTH fields on re-entry:
    - If current_node == last_completed_node → pipeline was between nodes; resume by dispatching next node after last_completed_node.
    - If current_node != last_completed_node → pipeline was inside current_node; read its cache file (or nodes_status):
        status PASS / FAIL / DEGRADED → already terminal (last_completed lag); proceed to next node.
        status WAITING_USER → re-present its 'question' to user, then re-dispatch with RESUME_MODE.
        status MESSAGE_PENDING → re-forward message, re-dispatch with RESUME_MODE.
        status missing / cache absent → re-dispatch fresh. (g) -> step4 NODE SUMMARY. (h) After step4 output, proceed to next node.
programExec.step5: After ALL nodes complete (or circuit breaker opens), return control to Engine Router for ExecutionSummary and conversation_context update. Engine does NOT write ExecutionSummary or update conversation_context -- Router handles these. Write final state to _index.json via MERGE using agent_update_index.py --updates (A1 v5.14.1 BUG FIX: MUST NOT overwrite _index.json directly — direct overwrite loses append-only fields like user_gates_presented, dispatch_records, dispatch_plan). Run: <python> -X utf8 {engine_dir}/python_tools/agent_update_index.py --cache_dir={cache_dir} --updates='{"execution_complete":true,"circuit_breaker_state":"...","last_completed_node":"..."}'.  The --updates flag performs JSON merge, preserving all existing fields. [PROHIBIT] Direct full-file write of _index.json at this step — use agent_update_index.py exclusively.
programExec.constraints: programExec reads target AIAP content from entry_path provided by Engine Router. Each node physically read from file. step4 NODE SUMMARY is MANDATORY after EVERY node -- the user is watching and expects to see progress for each node. You MUST output step4 BEFORE dispatching the next node in step3. If you skip NodeSummary, the user cannot track execution. Main Agent controls all user output directly. [PROHIBIT] Engine writing ExecutionSummary or conversation_context -- this is Router's responsibility. [PROHIBIT] Orchestrator hand-writing dispatch_plan -- generate_dispatch_plan.py is the SOLE authority for mode assignment (A2 v5.7.0). [PROHIBIT] Injecting behavioral directives into Sub Agent bootstrap (B1 v5.12.0, Axiom 0). DISPATCH AUDIT PRE-EXECUTION GATE (A3 v5.7.0): dispatch_audit.py --pre-execution MUST run after dispatch_plan generation and BEFORE first node dispatch. Violation -> block + force regeneration. NODES_IN_PATH COMPLETENESS CHECK (A1+A2 v5.13.1 branch-aware): dispatch_audit.py --completeness-check MUST run after dispatch audit pre-execution gate passes and BEFORE first node dispatch. Branch-aware: only walked branches checked, unwalked branches exempt. Gate node omission on walked branch -> CRITICAL (rebuild + re-audit, 2nd failure -> HALT). Non-gate omission on walked branch -> WARN (log, proceed). CRASH RECOVERY: always write last_completed_node atomically after each node. CIRCUIT BREAKER: 3 consecutive failures -> open. Half-open recovery: after 30s timeout (with up to 50% random jitter to avoid thundering herd on concurrent pipelines), allow 1 test dispatch. PASS -> closed. FAIL -> open again. TRANSIENT CACHE RETRY (A2 v5.6.1): max 1 retry per node for cache_write_failure/sub_agent_crash DEGRADED. Retry MUST use agent dispatch (never inline). Retry success does NOT increment consecutive_failure_count. DEGRADED CLASSIFICATION (A3 v5.6.1): all DEGRADED nodes must have degraded_reason enum + cache_write_attempted bool. PARALLEL DISPATCH: only for truly independent nodes (no data dependency). v5.0: NodeVerify (N1) runs after every cache verification. Decision Node gate (N2) routes branches based on mermaid diamond + cache route field. sys.* bootstrap hint (N3) included in Sub Agent bootstrap. Parallel wave interrupt handling (N1.5) processes WAITING_USER/MESSAGE_PENDING per-node within a wave. USER_GATE NODE VERIFY (X3 plan20/24, supersedes B2 v5.12.0 G4; v5.14.0): for ALL nodes at terminal status, ALWAYS run user_gate_audit.py --enforce (tool auto-detects gates via G1 text markers + G3 multi-file parsing); non-zero exit means the tool already coerced the node to WAITING_USER with FLAW-B precise steps_done/steps_remaining (resume re-presents + re-collects), so route as WAITING_USER per e2d — do NOT FAIL_HALT, do NOT hand-set status. The same --enforce backstop also runs inside agent_engine.writeCache.step3 (Layer-1, unified semantics). USER_GATES_PRESENTED (B4 v5.12.0): append-only _index.json field tracking WAITING_USER presentations; MUST be written BEFORE forwarding WAITING_USER question to user.
programExec.Error: retry(3) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(endNode with partial results) -> inform(User). CRITICAL. Error classification per error_taxonomy: transient [sub_agent_timeout, cache_write_timeout, file_read_timeout] -> retry with exponential backoff (base 1s, max 30s). fatal [scope_guard_violation, target_not_found, path_invalid (entry_path), GENERATION_PLAN_INTEGRITY_FAILURE] -> no retry, fallback immediately. recoverable_with_fallback [command_error, command_timeout, io_error] -> retry(1) then fallback.

endNode.step1: [ASSERT] programExec completed (or circuit breaker opened). | Output final response to user.
endNode.Error: retry(1) -> circuit-breaker -> fallback(inform User of incomplete execution) -> inform(User). OPTIONAL.
